items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu pays tribute to Lindsey Graham, "a great friend of Israel"
items.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called Graham a "great friend of Israel." "In our recent meeting, I said, 'Lindsey is a great friend of Israel and a cherished friend of mine. We have no better friend than Lindsey.' Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparable," he said in a statement released by his office. "He devoted his life to defending America, strengthening our alliance and standing up for the free world. Israel has lost one of its greatest friends." Our correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.

items.title: World Cup 2026 : England to face Argentina in tense semi-final
items.description: Switzerland meanwhile is eliminated after losing to Argentina 3-1. Mac Allister gave the reigning champions the lead only ten minutes into the game, heading the ball past Gregor Kobel off a Leo Messi cross. Switzerland gained in momentum, culminating in an equalizer from Dan Ndoye. But that goal was quickly overshadowed after Embolo was handed a second yellow for diving and was sent off. That obviously made the challenge even greater for Switzerland, but la Nati defended valiantly one man down, dragging Argentina into extra time. In the end though, Julian Alvarez scored a stunning goal curled in from outside the box. As Switzerland pushed for a second leveller, they were caught on the counter, and suffered a third and final goal at the hands of an unconvincing Argentinian side...

items.title: Argentina to repay US$4 billion, defying critics who doubted tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
items.description: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

items.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

items.title: Barbados leader rejects claim that ex-colonies should repay Britain
items.description: Barbados Prime Minister Mia Mottley called "asinine" a suggestion by a British lawmaker that Britain's former colonies should repay it for its historic investment in them.

items.title: Milei’s allied governors open door to suspension of PASO primaries
items.description: Government is seeking support to advance with its electoral reform push ahead of next voting cycle; Senate to have final say on potential suspension of compulsory primaries. Leer más

items.title: Typhoon Bavi weakens but still brings strong winds, rain to China
items.description: Strong winds and heavy rain are expected to impact many eastern and northeastern Chinese cities on Sunday (July 12) and Monday (July 13), China's National Meteorological Centre said

items.title: The week in pictures: Le Pen's comeback, Khamenei's funeral and Mbappé's wondergoal
items.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was cleared to run in next year's presidential election, despite having her embezzlement conviction confirmed, in a week that saw Iran hold days-long funeral ceremonies for slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and football stars Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi deliver moments of magic to carry their teams into the World Cup semi-finals. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

items.title: At least 15 Indian tourists dead after boat capsizes near island off Vietnam; rescue efforts under way
items.description: For any assistance, the first control room can be reached at: +84 36 281 7930, +84 91 552 37 14 and +84 33 452 0414; the other one in Hanoi can be reached at: +84 91 308 9165

items.title: Opposition rallies against Tshisekedi's third-term push
items.description: Political tensions are rising in the Democratic Republic of Congo as opposition leaders demand President Félix Tshisekedi's resignation and call for nationwide protests over proposed constitutional changes that could extend his time in power.

items.title: Bellingham scores twice to lift England past Haaland and Norway 2-1
items.description: Bellingham scored twice on Saturday — an equaliser in the first half and the go-ahead goal in the third minute of extra time — to lift England past Norway 2-1 and into the World Cup semi-finals for the first time since 2018. We discuss the match with our guest Robbie Nock, lead commentator and journalist.

items.title: Why did the U.S. indict Lawrence Bishnoi over Hardeep Singh Nijjar’s murder?
items.description: The U.S. has indicted Lawrence Bishnoi, a jailed Indian gangster, for his alleged role in a transnational conspiracy linked to the murder of Khalistani separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Canada in June 2023.

items.title: Delayed containment of Ebola could cost DRC and Uganda billions
items.description: Over US$700 million in additional health financing is needed to rapidly contain and prevent a wider regional crisis. The post Delayed containment of Ebola could cost DRC and Uganda billions appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
items.description: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking
items.description: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

items.title: The breaking of nations
items.description: From ancient times, periods of relative stability in which one big power was strong enough to maintain a semblance of order in the region it dominated have alternated with others marked by strife like the one we appear to be sliding into right now. Leer más

items.title: Newly-released video shows Peter Falconio's killer questioned
items.description: Australian police have released bodycam footage showing one of the last attempts to get Bradley Murdoch to reveal the location of Peter Falconio's body, on the 25th anniversary of his murder.

items.title: Jewish staff and students speak out as unis probed at antisemitism royal commission
items.description: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

items.title: WHO warns Sudan's cholera outbreak may worsen amid conflict and rains
items.description: The latest outbreak has claimed more than 100 lives so far and infected more than 1,300 others across several states.

items.title: Businesses caught between Labor's trust tax and stamp duty
items.description: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

items.title: US and its Latin American partners urge 'peaceful' transition in Colombia
items.description: In addition to Washington, signatories included Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guyana, Honduras, Panama, Paraguay, and Trinidad and Tobago. Leer más

items.title: California’s largest lake is turning to dust
items.description: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

items.title: UK Christians protest for Church of England to recognise genocide in Gaza
items.description: UK Christians are urging the Church of England General Synod to pass a motion to engage with Kairos Palelestine II

items.title: Anti-migrant display set alight in Northern Ireland bonfire
items.description: The flags of Palestine, Syria, Sudan and Somalia were draped over a huge bonfire tower before it was set alight.

items.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
items.description: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

items.title: Can ageless Lionel Messi keep delivering for Argentina at World Cup?
items.description: Aside from their diminutive genius, Argentina's most potent weapon is their remarkable camaraderie. The defending champions are now unbeaten in 11 World Cup games and the nation is dreaming of a fourth world title. Leer más

items.title: Thai zoo celebrates famous pygmy hippo's second birthday
items.description: In her enclosure at Khao Kheow Open Zoo in Chon Buri, a province east of Bangkok in Thailand, the 2-year-old Moo Deng approached the cake where her mother Jona had already begun digging in

items.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
items.description: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

items.title: Lawyers say Ebola patient placed in Equatorial Guinea hotel housing US deportees
items.description: Lawyers and two of the deportees say a man suspected of having the virus was brought to the hotel last week by people in hazmat suits.

items.title: Why the World Cup can be good for your mental health
items.description: The World Cup can offer more than just entertainment, it can also benefit your mental well-being. DW explains why.

items.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
items.description: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

items.title: 'This landscape is completely charred': Inside the village at epicentre of Spain's wildfires
items.description: The BBC's Nick Beake sees homes razed to the ground and "a sea of black" in Bédar.

items.title: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei laid to rest in Mashhad
items.description: Thousands of mourners performed funeral prayers Thursday on the final day of ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: ‘La cuarta estrella’: Argentina's players embrace fans' new World Cup anthem
items.description: 'La cuarta estrella' has swept through the stands before being adopted by the players themselves after their dramatic victory over Egypt, rekindling memories of Qatar 2022’s 'Muchachos.' Leer más

items.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
items.description: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.

items.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
items.description: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

items.title: Venezuela sets up temporary housing, as death toll hits 4,300
items.description: As the official death toll from the devastating earthquakes which struck northern Venezuela last month continues to rise, authorities are planning to set up temporary housing to shelter thousands of people left homeless.

items.title: Students abducted in May by Islamic militants in Nigeria recovering, governor says
items.description: Students abducted in May by Islamic militants in Nigeria’s southwestern state of Oyo were rescued and receiving medical treatment, confirmed the local government on Friday.

items.title: Young woman stopped taking the pill because of mental health concerns
items.description: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

items.title: Argentina's Lionel Scaloni says England World Cup semi 'just a football game'
items.description: "It is a football game and we will be playing against a very tough opponent. This is a football game and that's all," declares Argentina boss. Leer más

items.title: IMF welcomes Milei's reform proposal for Argentina's Central Bank
items.description: Multilateral lender welcomes Milei administration's proposal for Central Bank; IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva will visit Argentina on July 28 and 29. Leer más
